response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but his profile fundamentally mismatches this role's mandatory requirements. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. His career since has centered on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms and DLang systems work. He has no Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain experience (RIDE Capital was a Node.js fintech, not capital markets), and no stated SQL Server or Oracle depth. Solid fundamentals cannot substitute for the core mandatory stack and domain here.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and testing culture
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec)
Broad polyglot background with proven ability to pick up new stacks quickly
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, dated to 2015 and not Windows-platform focused
No Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain knowledge
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ experience vs. 5-10 year mandatory requirement
No capital markets / fixed income domain exposure
Strong engineering fundamentals and CI/CD practices
